summary: We are in the final sprint for the China Going-Global report launch and the Asia Start campaign, with the Knowledge Website redesign and Intelligent China program as parallel priorities.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint China Going-Global report with IE Business School, targeting a mid-September launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson in Singapore; a one-pager proposal is due from Felix this week. The Asia Start campaign is live with two routes (AI and traditional), and the Intelligent China program website needs completion. This week, urgent actions include marking feedback on the Knowledge Website redesign, finalizing the Shun Tian Shu press release, and following up with Media Minds for social media. Upcoming: the Global Program Brochure needs Dean Xiang's feedback by July 14, and the CKGSB-EuroCham event on EVs is being planned for July 15.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Redesign Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's knowledge website redesign, noting the menu categorization is improved but overall layout is too similar to current site. Joseph shared an alternative design with more articles displayed, and team discussed combining elements from both.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for China executive programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting - June 29, 2026
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, school brochure updates, CES quotes, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, social media agency scouting, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release approach and prioritizing Media Minds for social media.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses jointly publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools. They plan to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), discussed new Intelligent China program launching Dec 1-4 in Beijing, targeting foreigners with three modules.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market cooperation.

actionItems.detail: Sit down for half an hour to mark feedback on Joker's version for changes.

actionItems.text: Share HTML file of Joseph's design
actionItems.detail: Share the HTML file for the alternative design shown during meeting.

actionItems.detail: Need detailed description of each conversion event to verify tracking is correct.

actionItems.detail: Based on existing participant data and CRM leads to inform SEO and content strategy.

actionItems.text: Audit existing participant profiles from Asia Star and CRM
actionItems.detail: To understand target audience demographics and search behavior.

actionItems.detail: Julie to review and give feedback on menu categorization.

actionItems.detail: Xuan to join meeting at 3pm to discuss Google program brochure design.

actionItems.detail: Julie to send one ready article and work on rest this week.

actionItems.detail: Jimmy to adjust press release to incorporate 10 points or turn into 10-point article.

actionItems.detail: Kayleigh to get back to Carnegie account manager today regarding questions.

actionItems.detail: Kayleigh has a call with Impactable tomorrow night.

actionItems.detail: Team to wait for Li Wei to send sections to translate before dividing work.

actionItems.detail: To show to the president for approval.

actionItems.detail: For review and inclusion in the English report.

actionItems.text: Explore WeChat official accounts for alumni case studies to translate
actionItems.detail: To add industry perspective to the report.

actionItems.text: Confirm with Tech in Asia and Tyson about launching the report at their events
actionItems.detail: Meeting with Tech in Asia tomorrow to discuss session theme and launch possibility.

actionItems.text: Launch Asia Start campaign with two routes: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
actionItems.detail: Joseph to execute AI-driven route, Daisy to execute traditional strategy for first month.

actionItems.detail: Website nearly ready; Joseph to finalize and work with team.

actionItems.text: Plan SCM campaign for Intelligent China program.
actionItems.detail: Decide on route (AI or traditional) after webpage is done.

actionItems.detail: Xuan is on vacation until next Friday and will follow up on the partnership paperwork.

actionItems.meetingTitle: Re: CKGSB-EuroCham event collaboration on EV
actionItems.detail: Kaylee confirmed she will update Shengjia and start inviting Prof Li Wei for the event.

decisions.text: Videos will not have their own category; they will be dispersed by topic.
Knowledge menu should be centered, not left-aligned.
Indices (BCI, BSI) should be brought to the knowledge homepage.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; put US partnerships on hold initially.
Include Berkeley program as a third priority if resources allow.
Shun Tian Shu press release should be one piece, either incorporating 10 points or turning into 10-point article, not two separate pieces.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report will include main research from Professor Leeway, op-eds from President Santiago, Dean Hi, and possibly an industry perspective from an alum or case studies.
Target launch in mid-September at either Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will focus on module one (Beijing) initially, with option to buy modules separately.
Global Marcom's report is finalized and will be sent to Shouhuo
CKGSB-EuroCham event collaboration on EV will proceed with Prof Li Wei invited
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: CKGSB-EuroCham event collaboration on EV
